entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the demand for high-value food commodities is increasing and where smallholder farmers constitute a majority of the farm population

entities.name: HIGH-VALUE FOOD COMMODITIES
entities.description: Food products such as fruit, vegetables, milk, meat, fish, and eggs that have higher market value than staples

entities.name: FRUIT
entities.description: A type of high-value food commodity

entities.name: VEGETABLES
entities.description: A type of high-value food commodity

entities.name: FOOD GRAINS
entities.description: Basic food commodities compared against high-value food commodities in terms of demand

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers who represent over 81% of the total farm population in India

entities.name: HIGH-VALUE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agricultural production focused on high-value commodities, providing advantages in production and labor use

entities.name: STAPLES
entities.description: Basic food crops that high-value agriculture has a comparative advantage over

entities.name: INCOME AUGMENTATION
entities.description: The process of increasing income, for which high-value agriculture is a strategy

entities.name: EMPLOYMENT GENERATION
entities.description: The creation of jobs, for which high-value agriculture is a strategy

entities.name: URBANIZATION
entities.description: The process of urban growth that influences the demand for high-value food commodities

entities.name: INFRASTRUCTURE
entities.description: The physical and organizational structures that support the growth of high-value food commodities

relationships.description: Increasing demand for high-value food commodities in India
Fruit is a type of high-value food commodity
Vegetables are a type of high-value food commodity
Milk is a type of high-value food commodity
Meat is a type of high-value food commodity
Fish is a type of high-value food commodity
Eggs are a type of high-value food commodity
Demand for high-value food commodities is increasing faster than for food grains
Smallholder farmers make up over 81% of the farm population in India
High-value agriculture presents an opportunity and challenge for smallholder farmers
High-value agriculture has a comparative advantage over staples in production and labor use
High-value agriculture is a strategy for income augmentation
High-value agriculture is a strategy for employment generation
Urbanization plays a role in sustaining growth and demand for high-value food commodities
Infrastructure plays a role in sustaining growth and demand for high-value food commodities
